rationale: Codebase-pin check confirms every named symbol exists exactly where the bead and BRIEF cite it: Reviewer.model() at config.go:149 (BRIEF said ~139, off by 10 lines but same function/behavior), validateReviewerEntries at config.go:558 (BRIEF ~535/540, same margin), PanelGateReviewerSlots at config.go:795 and expandSlots at config.go:812, and TestLoad_EmptyStringModel at config_test.go:1496. `go test ./internal/config -run TestLoad_EmptyStringModel -v` PASSES both subtests. `go test ./internal/config` (fresh, -count=1) is all-green, no existing test broken. `go build ./...` succeeds with no output. Scope fence holds: `git show --name-only HEAD` lists exactly internal/config/config.go and internal/config/config_test.go; `Model  string` field type is unchanged; `supersedes` appears in both required doc comments (lines 144 and 553). git status is clean. No missing symbol, no red test, no build failure.
findings: Minor line-number drift only: Reviewer.model() is at config.go:149 vs BRIEF's ~139, and validateReviewerEntries is at config.go:558 vs BRIEF's ~535/540. Both are within normal review-brief tolerance and refer to the correct, unambiguous symbols — not a substantive finding.
